extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a becomes true only via rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is a 10-way conjunction that INCLUDES g6pd_deficiency. rec_drug_b becomes true via rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), whose antecedent is exactly g6pd_deficiency. Because the conjunction that sets rec_drug_a necessarily requires g6pd_deficiency=true, every input that makes rec_drug_a true also satisfies the trigger for rec_drug_b. The two rules write to different outputs (a vs b), so there is no priority/conflict resolution between them — both fire and set their outputs independently. Therefore any assignment with all 10 conjunction conditions true forces both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true, violating mutual exclusion. The defaults do not help since both outputs are explicitly set true. Concrete witness: all 10 conjunction inputs (g6pd_deficiency, immunosuppressed, anticoagulated, hypertension, low_ejection_fraction, qt_prolongation, elderly, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure, diabetes) = true; ckd_dialysis = false. Then rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true), so (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is true and the property is violated.
